log:
# Change log — A view on drug resistance in cancer

## 2026-06-27

Ingested the review "A view on drug resistance in cancer" (Vasan, Baselga & Hyman, Nature 575,
299-309, 2019; DOI:10.1038/s41586-019-1730-1) from `raw/source.md`.

- Created the `Publication` source node `Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer`
  (raw_source: raw/source.md).
- Authored 21 typed concept docs across Disease, Gene, Variant, Molecule, MolecularClass,
  BiomedicalMeasure, BiologicalPathway, MethodOrProcedure and Phenotype.
  - Diseases: Cancer drug resistance, Non-small-cell lung cancer, Melanoma, HER2-positive breast
    cancer.
  - Genes: EGFR, BRAF, KRAS, ESR1, BRCA1.
  - Variants: EGFR T790M, KRAS G12C.
  - Molecules (drugs/ADC): Osimertinib, Alectinib, Trastuzumab deruxtecan (DS-8201a), Olaparib,
    Venetoclax, Alpelisib.
  - MolecularClass: PARP inhibitor.
  - BiomedicalMeasure: Circulating tumour DNA (ctDNA).
  - BiologicalPathway: PI3K-AKT-mTOR, RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK.
  - MethodOrProcedure: CRISPR-Cas9 synthetic lethality screen.
  - Phenotype: Tumour heterogeneity.
- Stamped each claim edge with the provenance triplet (knowledge_level, agent_type,
  primary_source = the Publication node) and added reported_in back-links. Effect sizes/metrics put
  on edges where reported (e.g. ~60% olaparib remission at 3 years; PFS prolongation for osimertinib
  and alectinib).
name: Drug resistance in cancer

Alectinib.body:
# Alectinib

Potent ALK inhibitor that binds more potently to its target than first-generation ALK inhibitors.
It is active in crizotinib-resistant ALK-positive NSCLC and, as a first-line treatment, prolongs
progression-free survival compared with crizotinib — an example of using the more-potent agent
upfront to forestall resistance.

edges.note: prolongs progression-free survival vs crizotinib (first-generation ALK inhibitor); active in crizotinib-resistant disease
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Alpelisib.body:
# Alpelisib

PI3Kalpha-selective inhibitor with a low therapeutic index. As monotherapy it yields low response
rates, but combined with anti-endocrine therapy in ER+ PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer it improves
progression-free survival, at the cost of on-target hyperglycaemia from insulin inhibition.
Combining PI3K inhibition with a ketogenic diet (blunting adaptive insulin signalling) is proposed
to resensitize cells.

edges.note: PI3K inhibitor with low therapeutic index; improves PFS in combination but causes on-target hyperglycaemia from insulin inhibition
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.indication: ER+ PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer, combined with anti-endocrine therapy

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

BRAF.body:
# BRAF

Serine/threonine kinase oncogene in the RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK (MAP kinase) pathway. BRAF-V600E melanoma
responds to RAF/MEK inhibition but develops resistance through MAP-kinase pathway reactivation.
BRAF-mutant colorectal cancers are intrinsically insensitive to BRAF inhibitors because of feedback
reactivation of EGFR. Class 2 BRAF mutants signal as RAS-independent constitutive dimers that evade
ERK-dependent feedback and are only partially inhibited by existing allosteric MEK inhibitors.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: class 2 BRAF mutants signal as RAS-independent constitutive dimers only partially inhibited by allosteric MEK inhibitors

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

BRCA1.body:
# BRCA1

DNA-repair (homologous recombination) tumour-suppressor gene; with BRCA2 it underlies the classical
synthetic-lethal vulnerability to PARP inhibition in BRCA-mutant tumours. Acquired resistance to
PARP inhibitors can occur through secondary reversion mutations in BRCA1 or BRCA2 that restore
DNA-repair function. Patients with germline BRCA mutations are flagged as a high-risk population in
which ctDNA screening may be appropriate.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: secondary reversion mutations in BRCA1/BRCA2 restore homologous recombination and confer resistance to PARP inhibitors

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

CRISPR-Cas9 synthetic lethality screen.body:
# CRISPR-Cas9 synthetic lethality screen

Genome-scale CRISPR-Cas9 knockout screening identifies genes whose deletion confers sensitivity or
resistance to a given driver or drug. Relative to shRNA, CRISPR yields fewer off-target effects and
higher efficiency because a single-guide RNA can cleave both alleles. One of the first genome-wide
CRISPR knockout screens mapped mediators of resistance to RAF inhibition in BRAF-mutant melanoma,
and the approach now drives mapping of cancer dependencies and synthetic-lethal vulnerabilities,
including to immune checkpoint blockade.

edges.note: loss-of-function knockout screens identify genes whose deletion causes resistance or sensitivity to a driver or drug
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Cancer drug resistance.body:
# Cancer drug resistance

Drug resistance is the principal limiting factor to achieving cures in patients with cancer.
The review frames cancer therapy as a three-component system — a therapy that targets a population
of cancer cells within a particular host environment — and separates the biological determinants of
resistance: tumour burden and growth kinetics, tumour heterogeneity, physical barriers (sanctuary
sites, the blood-brain barrier), the immune system and microenvironment, undruggable drivers, and
the consequences of selective therapeutic pressure (adaptive responses and acquired resistance).

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Circulating tumour DNA.body:
# Circulating tumour DNA

ctDNA is tumour-derived DNA detectable in plasma — the basis of non-invasive "liquid biopsies."
The review presents it as a tool for early detection, depth-of-response quantification, and adaptive
monitoring of clonal evolution. After primary breast surgery, ctDNA detected minimal residual
disease eight months before imaging; in early NSCLC, tracking five mutations rather than one
improves detection; and ctDNA detection of EGFR T790M or emergent KRAS alleles guides therapy
switching. Low-burden detection is limited by template number, which is proportional to tumour
burden.

edges.note: non-invasive, dynamic, global detection of tumour burden and clonal evolution under therapy
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.note: tracking 5 mutations improves minimal-residual-disease detection vs 1 mutation in early-stage NSCLC
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

EGFR.body:
# EGFR

Receptor tyrosine kinase oncogene. Mutant EGFR drives a subset of NSCLC. Acquired resistance to
first-generation EGFR TKIs is dominated by the EGFR T790M gatekeeper mutation, overcome by the
third-generation irreversible inhibitor osimertinib. Resistance can also arise by MET amplification
(activating ERBB3 signalling). In BRAF-mutant colorectal cancer, feedback reactivation of EGFR
confers insensitivity to BRAF inhibition — a phenomenon largely absent in melanoma, which expresses
low EGFR.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: MET amplification activates ERBB3 signalling downstream of EGFR, driving resistance to EGFR inhibitors

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

EGFR T790M.body:
# EGFR T790M

The most common acquired-resistance mutation in EGFR-mutant NSCLC after first-generation EGFR
inhibitors. This gatekeeper threonine-to-methionine substitution sterically blocks ATP-competitive
TKIs. T790M is detectable in plasma ctDNA — now standard of care for patients progressing on
gefitinib or erlotinib — and confers sensitivity to the mutant-selective irreversible inhibitor
osimertinib.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: gatekeeper mutation blocks access of ATP-competitive first-generation TKIs to the kinase domain

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

ESR1.body:
# ESR1

Gene encoding the oestrogen receptor (ER), a nuclear hormone receptor target in breast cancer.
Subclonal driver mutations in ESR1 — detectable in circulating tumour DNA — drive resistance to
endocrine therapy. Characteristic acquired mutations in the ligand-binding domain produce
ligand-independent (constitutive) transcription, a recurrent mechanism of hormone-resistant breast
cancer.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: ligand-binding-domain mutations cause ligand-independent transcription and endocrine resistance

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

HER2-positive breast cancer.body:
# HER2-positive breast cancer

Breast cancer with amplification of the HER2 (ERBB2) receptor. The review highlights that the
combination of the synergistic anti-HER2 antibodies trastuzumab and pertuzumab markedly improves
survival, and that the modern HER2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate trastuzumab deruxtecan
(DS-8201a) shows durable efficacy even in HER2-low breast cancer, where HER2 amplification is below
the threshold for response to conventional anti-HER2 therapy.

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

KRAS.body:
# KRAS

RAS-family GTPase oncogene, historically "undruggable" alongside MYC and TP53. Downstream KRAS- or
NRAS-activating mutations confer resistance to anti-EGFR antibody therapy in colorectal cancer
(target indifference). The allele-specific covalent inhibitor approach to the KRAS(G12C) mutant —
trapping and inactivating the mutant protein — is presented as a route to drugging a previously
intractable target.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: downstream KRAS- or NRAS-activating mutations mediate resistance to anti-EGFR therapy in colorectal cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

KRAS G12C.body:
# KRAS G12C

Glycine-to-cysteine substitution at codon 12 of KRAS. Long considered undruggable, KRAS(G12C) is
targetable by allele-specific covalent inhibitors that exploit the reactive cysteine to trap and
inactivate the mutant protein by controlling GTP affinity and effector interactions — cited as proof
that previously intractable oncogenic drivers can be drugged.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: historically undruggable RAS driver; allele-specific covalent inhibitors trap the mutant in its inactive GDP-bound state

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Melanoma.body:
# Melanoma

Metastatic melanoma is a paradigm tumour for both targeted and immune therapy in the review.
BRAF-V600E melanoma is treated with RAF/MEK inhibitors, but acquires resistance through MAP-kinase
pathway reactivation identified by gain-of-function and CRISPR knockout screens. Melanoma is also a
leading responder to checkpoint blockade; acquired resistance to PD-1 blockade arises through JAK1/
JAK2 mutations and loss of beta-2-microglobulin, which impair interferon-gamma response and antigen
presentation.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Non-small-cell lung cancer.body:
# Non-small-cell lung cancer

NSCLC is a recurring example in the review. EGFR-mutant NSCLC initially responds to first-generation
EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors but develops acquired resistance, most commonly via the EGFR T790M
gatekeeper mutation, which is targeted by osimertinib. ALK-positive NSCLC responds to alectinib,
which prolongs progression-free survival versus first-generation ALK inhibitors. Treatment-induced
transformation of EGFR-mutant NSCLC to small-cell-like neuroendocrine phenotypes (with loss of RB1
and TP53) is a histological mechanism of resistance.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Olaparib.body:
# Olaparib

PARP inhibitor that exploits synthetic lethality in homologous-recombination-deficient (BRCA-mutant)
tumours. Two years of maintenance olaparib in newly diagnosed germline BRCA-mutant ovarian cancer
deepens responses after platinum-based chemotherapy, with a remission rate of about 60% at three
years — suggesting that early, aggressive use increases the fraction of patients cured. Acquired
resistance can arise via BRCA reversion mutations.

edges.note: two years of maintenance olaparib drives deeper, more durable responses; ~60% remission at 3 years
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.indication: germline BRCA-mutant ovarian cancer, maintenance after platinum-based chemotherapy
stats.timepoint: 3 years after start of PARP inhibitor therapy

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Osimertinib.body:
# Osimertinib

Third-generation, mutant-selective, irreversible EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor. It overcomes
on-target acquired resistance mediated by the EGFR T790M gatekeeper mutation and, used first-line,
prolongs progression-free survival in EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC compared with first-generation
EGFR inhibitors.

edges.note: prolongs progression-free survival vs first-generation EGFR inhibitors as first-line treatment
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: irreversible, mutant-selective binding to the EGFR kinase domain

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

PARP inhibitor.body:
# PARP inhibitor

Pharmacologic class of drugs (e.g. olaparib, niraparib) that inhibit poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase.
The classical synthetic-lethal therapy: PARP inhibition kills BRCA-mutant cells whose homologous
recombination DNA-repair pathway is already defective. Maintenance PARP inhibition deepens responses
in BRCA-mutant ovarian cancer; reversion mutations in BRCA1/BRCA2 confer acquired resistance.

edges.note: classical exploitation of synthetic lethality with BRCA-mutant DNA-repair defects
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

PI3K-AKT-mTOR signaling pathway.body:
# PI3K-AKT-mTOR signaling pathway

A core growth-signalling cascade (HER2-PI3K-AKT-mTOR) that is critical for both normal and cancer
cells and is heavily regulated by feedback. Targeting it almost always elicits adaptive responses:
mTOR or AKT inhibition relieves feedback suppression of receptor tyrosine kinases, and PI3Kalpha
inhibition in ER+ PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer triggers epigenetic reprogramming that increases ER
transcription. Subclonal mutations in PI3K-pathway genes (and PTEN loss driving PI3Kbeta signalling)
drive resistance to targeted therapy.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: feedback-driven adaptive reactivation (relief of negative feedback) is a prevalent resistance mechanism when this pathway is targeted

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK signaling pathway.body:
# RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK signaling pathway

The MAP kinase signalling cascade through which RAS, BRAF, MEK and ERK transmit proliferative
signals. Targeting it (RAF/MEK inhibitors) triggers profound ERK-dependent feedback relief, and
acquired resistance in BRAF-V600E melanoma converges on ERK-dependent, ERK-independent and
pathway-indifferent reactivation states identified by gain-of-function and CRISPR knockout screens.

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: ERK-dependent feedback relief and pathway reactivation confer adaptive and acquired resistance to RAF/MEK inhibitors

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Trastuzumab deruxtecan.body:
# Trastuzumab deruxtecan

A modern HER2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate (ADC): a monoclonal anti-HER2 antibody covalently
linked to a cytotoxic payload. It delivers a chemotherapy dose that would be far too toxic if given
systemically, directly to tumour cells. DS-8201a shows durable efficacy in HER2-low breast cancer,
demonstrating that a modern ADC can deliver chemotherapy based on low levels of target expression
even when the target is not an oncogene for that tumour.

edges.note: durable efficacy in HER2-low breast cancer where HER2 amplification is below the threshold for response to conventional anti-HER2 therapy
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Tumour heterogeneity.body:
# Tumour heterogeneity

Spatial and temporal genetic diversity within a tumour, generated by mutational processes (age-related
mutations, APOBEC editing, genomic instability, chromothripsis, chromosomal instability). It is
perhaps the most easily conceptualized cause of drug resistance: targeting an "actionable" driver is
only effective if the mutation is truncal (clonal). Subclonal driver mutations — e.g. in ESR1 or the
PI3K pathway — are sufficient to drive resistance, motivating a catalogue of driver "clonality."

edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

stats.mechanism: spatial and temporal genetic diversity from mutational processes; subclonal driver mutations drive resistance, and targeting non-truncal mutations fails

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance i.body:
# A view on drug resistance in cancer

Review article (Nature, 13 November 2019) by Neil Vasan, José Baselga and David M. Hyman.
It takes a reductionist approach to the biological determinants of drug resistance in cancer —
tumour burden and growth kinetics; tumour heterogeneity; physical barriers; the immune system
and microenvironment; undruggable cancer drivers; and the consequences of therapeutic pressure —
and proposes four general solutions: earlier detection (cancer interception), deeper responses,
adaptive therapeutic monitoring, and exploitation of cancer dependencies via synthetic-lethality
screens and computational modelling.

This Publication node is the ingested source for this bundle; its bytes live at `raw/source.md`.

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance i.identifier: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
synonyms: A view on drug resistance in cancer

Venetoclax.body:
# Venetoclax

BCL2 inhibitor and a unique example of a low-therapeutic-index agent: by lowering the apoptotic
threshold of cells it produces very deep responses in chronic lymphocytic leukaemia. Its on-target
liability — dramatic tumour lysis syndrome in patients with high disease burden — is managed with
careful dose escalation.

edges.note: lowers the apoptotic threshold of cells and produces very deep responses in CLL
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer

edges.object: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
edges.primary_source: Vasan 2019 — A view on drug resistance in cancer
